User.title: User
User.description: Represents a customer account in the MechConnect application. This entity stores personal profile information for customers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for contact and potentially linked to an external authentication system.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Workshop.title: Workshop
Workshop.description: Represents a mechanical workshop registered in the MechConnect application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Workshop entity.

name.description: The official name of the workshop.

description.description: A brief description of the workshop and its specialties.

address.description: The physical street address of the workshop.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the workshop's location, for map display.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the workshop's location, for map display.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the workshop.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the workshop.

website.description: The official website URL for the workshop (optional).

openingHours.description: An array of strings describing the daily opening hours of the workshop (e.g., 'Monday: 09:00-17:00', 'Saturday: 10:00-14:00').

serviceIds.description: References to Service IDs offered by this workshop. (Relationship: Workshop N:N Service)

hasObdiiScannerService.description: Indicates if the workshop explicitly offers OBDII scanner services, useful for filtering and highlighting.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the workshop profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the workshop profile was last updated.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a type of mechanical service that workshops can offer and customers can book.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Oil Change', 'OBDII Scan', 'Brake Inspection').

description.description: A detailed description of the service.

isObdiiScannerService.description: Indicates if this service specifically involves an OBDII scanner.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a customer's booking for a specific service at a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the User who booked the appointment. (Relationship: User 1:N Appointment)

workshopId.description: Reference to the Workshop where the appointment is booked. (Relationship: Workshop 1:N Appointment)

serviceId.description: Reference to the specific Service requested for this appointment. (Relationship: Service 1:N Appointment)

scheduledDateTime.description: The planned date and time for the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

customerNotes.description: Optional notes or specific requests provided by the customer for the appointment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches the document ID (path parameter).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores details of mechanical workshops. Publicly readable for all users. Write access is restricted to the workshop owner. This document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier of the workshop.

definition.description: Stores global definitions of mechanical services. Publicly readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the service.

definition.description: Stores appointments booked by a customer at a workshop. Access is granted to the `customerId` (from path) and the `workshopOwnerId`. This document includes denormalized 'customerId' and 'workshopOwnerId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (customer) who booked the appointment.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the appointment.

definition.description: Serves as an authorization collection. The existence of a document at this path grants administrative privileges to the corresponding user (userId). This utilizes an 'existence over content' pattern for DBAC.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user with administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for MechConnect prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure robust security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (CRITICAL):**
To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, key authorization contexts are denormalized:
*   **Workshops:** Each `workshop` document will include an `ownerId` field (referencing a `User`'s UID). This denormalization allows rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` directly when a workshop owner attempts to modify their workshop, without needing to traverse a complex hierarchy or make additional reads.
*   **Appointments:** Each `appointment` document, placed under a user's subcollection (`/users/{customerId}/appointments/{appointmentId}`), will include `customerId` (which is also the path parameter, but explicit in the document for clarity and collection group query rules) and `workshopOwnerId` (denormalized from the associated `Workshop` document). This ensures that both the customer and the workshop owner can be authorized to read/write the appointment directly from the `appointment` document itself, using rules like `request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerId` OR `request.auth.uid == resource.data.workshopOwnerId`.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs:**
Collections are designed with homogeneous security postures, simplifying security rules and supporting efficient query access patterns (QAPs):
*   **`/users/{userId}`:** This collection stores private user profiles. Only the authenticated user matching `{userId}` can read or write their own profile. This inherently supports QAPs as a user only queries their own specific document.
*   **`/workshops/{workshopId}`:** This collection is publicly readable, allowing any user to browse workshops for location, services, etc. Write access is restricted to the `ownerId` stored within each `workshop` document. This supports QAPs for public listings and owner-specific updates.
*   **`/services/{serviceId}`:** This collection provides global, read-only definitions of available services. It is publicly readable by all. Write access would be restricted to administrators (managed via `/roles_admin`). This supports QAPs for listing all available services.
*   **`/users/{customerId}/appointments/{appointmentId}`:** Appointments are placed hierarchically under the customer who booked them. This makes it trivial for a customer to retrieve their own appointments (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/appointments`). For workshops to view their relevant appointments, a collection group query on `appointments` will be used, with rules filtering by the denormalized `workshopOwnerId`. This structure effectively supports QAPs for both customers and workshop owners.
*   **`/roles_admin/{userId}`:** This collection uses an 'existence over content' pattern for administrative roles. A document's presence for a specific `userId` grants admin privileges. This is crucial for securely managing global resources like `Service` definitions or potentially all `Workshop` documents.
